If false, the system disables summarization of content in Safari

ConfigurationProfile.description: Component for sending any Apple MDM configuration profile payload as a blueprint component.

identifier.description:
## Using Apple MDM profile payloads as blueprint components

The API accepts **any** Apple MDM configuration profile payload as a
blueprint component — not only the ones explicitly listed in this
specification. Use the `com.jamf.ddm-configuration-profile` identifier with the
pattern described here to send one or more Apple payloads.

Apple publishes all payload definitions at
[apple/device-management/mdm/profiles](https://github.com/apple/device-management/tree/release/mdm/profiles).

### Step 1 — Choose the Apple payload(s)

Find the payload YAML in the Apple repository. The `payloadtype` field
(e.g. `com.apple.domains`, `com.apple.wifi.managed`,
`com.apple.applicationaccess`) identifies each payload.

> **Unsupported payload types:** Blueprints with
> `com.apple.font` or `com.apple.webClip.managed` payloads
> cannot be created.

### Step 2 — Build the `configuration` object

The `configuration` has two mandatory fields:

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `payloadDisplayName` | string | A human-readable name shown on the device (e.g. `"Managed Domains"`) |
| `payloadContent` | array | An array of payload objects — each one represents a single Apple payload |

Each entry in `payloadContent` **must** contain:

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `payloadType` | string | The Apple payload type identifier (e.g. `com.apple.domains`) |

In addition to `payloadType`, include the payload-specific keys from the
`payloadkeys` section of the Apple payload YAML.

### Apple type → JSON type mapping

| Apple type | JSON value type | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `<string>` | string | `"example.com"` |
| `<integer>` | number | `42` |
| `<real>` | number | `3.14` |
| `<boolean>` | boolean | `true` |
| `<data>` | string (Base64-encoded) | `"SGVsbG8="` |
| `<date>` | string (ISO 8601) | `"2026-01-15T00:00:00Z"` |
| `<array>` | array | `["a", "b"]` |
| `<dictionary>` | object | `{ "key": "value" }` |

Only include the keys you want to configure. Keys marked
`presence: optional` in the Apple YAML can be omitted; keys marked
`presence: required` must be present.

### Reference

- Apple payload definitions: https://github.com/apple/device-management/tree/release/mdm/profiles
- Apple Configuration Profile reference: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/devicemanagement/profile-specific-payload-keys

ConfigurationProfileConfiguration.description:
Configuration wrapper for one or more Apple MDM profile payloads.

### Example — `com.apple.domains`

Using the payload defined in
[com.apple.domains.yaml](https://github.com/apple/device-management/blob/release/mdm/profiles/com.apple.domains.yaml),
its `payloadkeys` are `EmailDomains`, `WebDomains`,
`SafariPasswordAutoFillDomains`, `CrossSiteTrackingPreventionRelaxedDomains`,
and `CrossSiteTrackingPreventionRelaxedApps` — all optional arrays of strings.

```json
{
  "payloadDisplayName": "Managed Domains",
  "payloadContent": [
    {
      "payloadType": "com.apple.domains",
      "EmailDomains": ["example.com", "private.example.com"],
      "WebDomains": ["*.example.com"],
      "SafariPasswordAutoFillDomains": ["login.example.com"]
    }
  ]
}
```

### Example — `com.apple.wifi.managed`

The same pattern works for any payload. For
[com.apple.wifi.managed](https://github.com/apple/device-management/blob/release/mdm/profiles/com.apple.wifi.managed.yaml),
look up its `payloadkeys` and send:

```json
{
  "payloadDisplayName": "Corporate Wi-Fi",
  "payloadContent": [
    {
      "payloadType": "com.apple.wifi.managed",
      "SSID_STR": "Corporate-WiFi",
      "EncryptionType": "WPA2",
      "AutoJoin": true,
      "IsHotspot": false
    }
  ]
}
```

### Combining multiple Apple payloads in one component

A single `com.jamf.ddm-configuration-profile` component can contain multiple
Apple payloads in the `payloadContent` array:

```json
{
  "payloadDisplayName": "Network & Domains",
  "payloadContent": [
    {
      "payloadType": "com.apple.domains",
      "WebDomains": ["*.corp.example.com"],
      "EmailDomains": ["corp.example.com"]
    },
    {
      "payloadType": "com.apple.wifi.managed",
      "SSID_STR": "CorpNet",
      "EncryptionType": "WPA3"
    }
  ]
}
```

You can also place multiple `com.jamf.ddm-configuration-profile` components in the
same blueprint step alongside other component types.

payloadContent.description:
An array of Apple payload objects. Each entry represents a single
Apple MDM payload and **must** include a `payloadType` field set to
the Apple payload type identifier (e.g. `com.apple.domains`,
`com.apple.wifi.managed`, `com.apple.applicationaccess`).

In addition to `payloadType`, include any payload-specific keys from
the `payloadkeys` section of the corresponding Apple payload YAML
definition. Any additional properties beyond `payloadType` are
accepted to accommodate any Apple payload schema.

payloadDisplayName.description: A human-readable name for this configuration profile, displayed on the device.

ConfigurationProfileContentItem.description:
A single Apple MDM payload entry. Must contain `payloadType` to
identify the Apple payload, plus any payload-specific keys.

description.example: Keep all eligible MacOS devices updated to the last major version

Description.description: Map of OS language ID to a localized description. Use the special `default` key for languages that the dictionary doesn't contain.

DetailsUrl.description: Optional URL with details about the enforced update

Included.description: Controls whether this setting should be used

Value.description: URL of a web page with the details about the enforced update

DiskManagementSettingsConfiguration.description:
Disk management settings configuration.

Configures restrictions for external and network storage access.

code.description: Error-specific code that can be used to identify localization string, etc.

description.description: A general description of error for troubleshooting/debugging. Generally this text should not be displayed to a user; instead refer to errorCode and it's localized text.

field.description: Name of the field that caused the error.

Folder.description: An array of bookmarks for each bookmark in the folder

Title.description: The title of the folder shown in Safari

Included.description: Controls whether this control should be used.

RPN.description: Configures whether RPN input is enabled.

UnitConversion.description: Configures whether unit conversions are enabled.

Enabled.description: Controls whether the mode is enabled.

Included.description: Controls whether this control should be used.

MathSettingsConfiguration.description:
Math settings configuration.

Configures Calculator app modes and system-wide math behavior including keyboard suggestions and Math Notes.

Meta.description: Meta object containing additional information about component

ExtensionIdentifier.description: The composed identifier of the extension that provides the start page

HomepageURL.description: The URL of the homepage which needs to start with https:// or http://

PageType.description: Sets the start page type in Safari

Included.description: Controls whether this control should be used.
Controls whether this control should be used.

totalCount.description: Total number of items matching the filter
Total number of items matching the filter

PasscodeSettingsConfiguration.description:
Passcode settings configuration.

Configures device passcode requirements including complexity, length, and expiration.

Enabled.description: Controls whether the mode is enabled.

Included.description: Controls whether this control should be used.
Controls whether this control should be used.

SafariBookmarksConfiguration.description:
Safari bookmarks configuration.

Defines managed bookmark groups and folders that appear in Safari across managed devices.

ManagedBookmarks.description: A dictionary that specifies a set of managed bookmarks

SafariExtensionsConfiguration.description:
Safari extensions configuration.

Manages Safari browser extensions including allowed/denied domains and private browsing settings.

SafariSettingsConfiguration.description:
Safari settings configuration.

Configures Safari browser behavior including cookies, JavaScript, popups, private browsing, and start page settings.

Enabled.description: Controls whether the mode is enabled.

Included.description: Controls whether this control should be used.

SoftwareUpdateSettingsConfiguration.description:
Software update settings configuration.

Configures OS update behavior including notifications, automatic actions, deferrals, and rapid security responses.

Included.description: Controls whether this setting should be used

Value.description: Controls whether the mode is enabled.

SwUpdateAutomaticConfiguration.description:
Automatic software update enforcement configuration.

Automatically enforces updates based on strategy (LATEST or SEMANTIC).

strategy.description: Strategy for enforcing automatic OS updates

SwUpdateConfiguration.description:
Software update enforcement configuration.

Configures OS update enforcement using either manual (specific version by date) or automatic strategies.

enforcementType.description: Type of software update enforcement strategy

SwUpdateLatestConfiguration.description:
Automatic software update with LATEST strategy.
Enforces the newest available OS version a specified number of days after release.

deploymentTime.description: The device local time (HH:MM) when the update should be installed

enforceAfterDays.description: The number of days after the OS update release

SwUpdateManualConfiguration.description:
Manual software update enforcement configuration.

Enforces a specific OS version by a specific date/time.

targetLocalDateTime.description: Local time of the device until which update must be performed

targetOSVersion.description: Target OS version in semantic versioning format

SwUpdateSemanticConfiguration.description:
Automatic software update with SEMANTIC strategy.
Applies semantic versioning rules to determine update enforcement timing.

Included.description: Controls whether this control should be used.

KeyboardSuggestions.description: Controls whether keyboard suggestions include math solutions.

MathNotes.description: Controls whether Math Notes is allowed in other apps such as Notes.

Disabled.description: If true, temporary pairing of audio accessories is disabled

Included.description: Controls whether this setting should be used

Hour.description: Local time hour (24-hour clock) for automatic unpairing. Required when Policy is "Hour".

Policy.description: A string that specifies the device's unpairing policy.

info.description: Blueprints simplify the creation of complex workflows by leveraging declarative device management to ensure devices meet a particular management state. You can use blueprints to scope management settings to devices using customizable components that include payloads and their configurable settings, all in one location.
info.title: Blueprints API

**Required Permissions:** `read:pro:blueprints`, `read:school:blueprints`

Use `desc` for Descending ordering. Additional sort parameters are supported and determine order of results that have equivalent values for previous sort parameters.
